prompt: Construct a WildLifeLens Operations Suite supporting offline member engagement, staff time validation, photographer scheduling, and order-to-fulfillment execution for a regional wildlife-park gift shop and photo desk. Primary roles include Members (customers), Front Counter Staff, Photographers, Kitchen/Prep Staff using a back-of-house KDS screen, and Administrators. The user experience is built with HTMX to keep screens fast and responsive without a SPA: members can be looked up by phone number or member ID (no SMS), view tier benefits, points balance, and stored-value balance, and redeem points at checkout with immediate on-screen confirmation and a printed receipt note. Staff can create orders, apply discounts limited by tier, and move orders through clear statuses from created to paid to in-prep to ready to delivered and reviewed, with visible countdown badges; unpaid orders auto-cancel after 30 minutes, and paid-but-unclaimed orders move to “ready for pickup” and expire after 4 hours for end-of-day reconciliation. Photographers manage availability with a calendar-like view to mark working windows, days off, and breaks; front counter can book a time slot, see conflicts in real time, and lock a slot for 5 minutes while collecting customer details. A high-performance search screen supports keyword and synonym/pinyin fuzzy matching with highlighted hits and smart filters such as taxonomy, region, habitat, size range, and protection level to quickly locate products, photo packages, or educational items; recent searches and trending terms are shown locally on the device to speed repeat transactions. Staff clock in/out from a dedicated kiosk view with immediate pass/fail feedback and a missed-punch correction form routed to admin review. The backend uses Flask to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by HTMX partial updates, keeping the frontend and backend decoupled while staying fully offline. SQLite provides durable local persistence for member profiles, tier rules, point ledgers, stored-value ledgers, orders, order events, KDS tickets, schedules, and audit logs. Unified identity is enforced with locally verified usernames and salted password hashes, plus server-side sessions or JWT stored locally; authorization uses hybrid RBAC/ABAC so an admin can configure roles and data scope (by location, station, or employee) down to menu, API, field, and record level, with all permission changes written to an immutable audit trail. The clock-in validation pipeline runs locally: it performs a configurable face-match threshold (default 0.85) against an on-device reference photo captured at enrollment, checks environment quality (minimum brightness and single-face requirement), records a device fingerprint, rate-limits punch attempts to 3 per 5 minutes, and produces a tamper-evident punch credential by hashing the punch payload with a server secret and storing the signature. Points policies are rule-driven: points earn at 1 point per $1.00 pre-tax, redemption capped at 20% of an order subtotal, points expire 365 days after issuance, and high-risk behavior (over 10 redemptions per day or over $200.00 stored-value spend per day) triggers a local review flag that blocks further redemptions until an administrator clears it. The order state machine validates allowed transitions, logs every milestone with timestamp and actor, supports export to CSV for end-of-day close, and routes KDS tickets to stations by item category with priority, ETA, completion confirmation write-back, and local critical alerts on the KDS screen (e.g., “late by 10 minutes” or “allergy note present”) without relying on any external services. Sensitive fields such as stored-value balances and member contact info are encrypted at rest using an application-managed key and masked in staff views unless the user has explicit permission.
